name: Eagle Jewelry and Loan
description_short: Eagle Jewelry and Loan is a Houston-based pawn shop specializing in pre-owned jewelry, watches, and accessories since 1995. They offer in-person transactions and online layaway payments.
description_long: Eagle Jewelry and Loan has operated in Houston, Texas since 1995, establishing itself as a community-focused pawn shop with membership in the Better Business Bureau and the National Pawnbrokers Association. The company positions itself as a relationship-driven business emphasizing availability and customer care within the local community. Their core business model centers on buying, selling, and pawning pre-owned jewelry, watches, and accessories. They explicitly clarify they are not affiliated with luxury brands like Rolex or Cartier, indicating they deal in second-hand and vintage items rather than new luxury goods. The company operates a physical retail location at 6222 Richmond Ave, Suite 150 in Houston with standard business hours Monday through Saturday, supplemented by an online presence for layaway payments. Their website emphasizes personal service and invites customers to visit during business hours, reflecting the traditional pawn shop model that relies on in-person evaluation and transactions. Eagle Jewelry and Loan differentiates itself through BBB and industry association membership, suggesting attention to business standards and transparency. However, the company provides minimal information about loan terms, interest rates, lending procedures, or specific services beyond product sales and layaway options. The website lacks details about collateral-based lending processes, redemption policies, or typical loan amounts, which are standard disclosures for pawn operations. As a pawn shop rather than a consumer finance lender, Eagle Jewelry and Loan serves a limited niche focused primarily on jewelry and accessory sales with secondary pawn services. The business model depends on foot traffic and local market awareness rather than broader financial services.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: BBB member and National Pawnbrokers Association member, indicating industry accountability
Nearly 30-year operating history in Houston since 1995 establishes stability
Online layaway payment option available for customers unable to visit in person
Clear transparency about being non-affiliated with luxury brands like Rolex or Cartier
Published business hours with Saturday availability for working customers
Active community engagement emphasized in marketing materials
Newsletter signup offers 10% discount for first-time customers
cons: No published interest rates, loan terms, or fee structure for pawn transactions
Limited online functionality—primarily informational with no e-commerce or loan applications
Website provides no details about typical loan amounts, redemption policies, or collateral requirements
No information about alternative payment methods or financial products beyond pawn/sale
Missing key consumer disclosures standard for regulated lending operations
best_for: Houston-area residents seeking to sell or pawn jewelry, watches, or luxury accessories
Customers preferring in-person transactions with face-to-face evaluation
Buyers looking for pre-owned or vintage jewelry at discount prices
diagnosis: Eagle Jewelry and Loan is best suited for Houston residents seeking to buy, sell, or pawn jewelry and watches with traditional in-person service. The main caveat is that they operate as a retail pawn shop with minimal online functionality and no published loan terms—customers must visit in person or call for specific rates and collateral information.
services: Jewelry and watch appraisal and evaluation
Pawn loans on jewelry and accessories
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
meta_description: Eagle Jewelry and Loan: Houston pawn shop specializing in pre-owned jewelry, watches, and accessories since 1995 with online layaway.
